en.extract:
The streaked rosefinch (Carpodacus rubicilloides) is a true finch species (family Fringillidae). It is found on the Himalayan Plateau. Its natural habitat is boreal shrubland.
Two subspecies are recognised:

C. r. rubicilloides - eastern Tibet to central and southern China
C. r. lucifer - southern Tibet and the Himalayas

The '''streaked rosefinch''' ('''''Carpodacus rubicilloides''''') is a [[true finch]] [[species]] ([[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Fringillidae]]). It is found on the [[Himalayan Plateau]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is boreal [[shrubland]].

Two subspecies are recognised:<ref name=iou>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | title=Finches, euphonias | work= World Bird List Version 5.2| url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/finches/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union| access-date=5 June 2015 }}</ref>
*''C. r. rubicilloides'' - eastern Tibet to central and southern China
*''C. r. lucifer'' - southern Tibet and the Himalayas
